description_long: This object is a traditional spinning wheel, constructed primarily from dark-stained wood with a visibly aged patina. It features a large, spoked drive wheel mounted vertically on one side, connected to a horizontal wooden frame supported by three turned legs. The flyer assembly, with two upright posts and a horizontal spindle, is mounted at the front end of the frame, while a treadle bar is attached near the base for foot operation. The wood shows signs of wear and use, with some areas appearing smoother and slightly lighter from handling. The overall design is ornate yet functional, with turned wood details and a triangular base for stability. This spinning wheel would typically be found in historical or decorative urban settings, such as museums, antique shops, or themed parks.
description: A vintage wooden spinning wheel with a large spoked drive wheel, ornate turned legs, and a horizontal frame supporting a flyer and treadle assembly.
description_view_0: The large spoked drive wheel and the flyer assembly are clearly visible, along with the ornate turned legs and the treadle bar at the base.
description_view_1: A side-on view showing the narrow profile of the spinning wheel, with the drive wheel edge-on and the flyer assembly and treadle bar aligned.
description_view_2: The drive wheel dominates the view, with the flyer assembly and frame visible at an angle, highlighting the triangular base and leg structure.
description_view_3: A direct side view with the drive wheel and flyer assembly in profile, showing the alignment of the legs and the treadle bar beneath.
